safest_plan.entry_basis: Lower end of the 16 Jun 'spot accumulation 80-90' grade, which lines up with the chart's weekly-demand band (~77-80). Buying the bottom of the zone rather than the 90 level that has already been broken below.

targets.basis: 115$ 'Partial TP' red band — labelled on BOTH charts
124$ 'More consistent TP' at the 0.786/1.618 fib — labelled on BOTH charts
16 Jun grade macro 3-4x target (150-155 red band, newest chart only)

safest_plan.stop_basis: A daily close below ~75 loses the weekly-demand base / macro support that the 16 Jun chart flags as the last line; stop sits just under that band.

stance_by_source.summary: Still wants to accumulate Litecoin spot in the 80-90 area for a swing higher, but the protective stop already got run, the setup was downgraded to 'partial', and he's now watching the deeper ~77 area in case of a bigger flush before the move up.
Textbook discounted buy at the 90 zone (with the 9-month demand base near 70 as the backstop), aiming first for 115 then 124.

conflicts: The written entry calls 90 the '9-month demand', but both charts clearly put the 9-month demand band down at ~68-72 — 90 is the discounted entry zone, not the demand base. The image wins.

Between 19 May and 16 June the call weakened rather than reversed: the earlier 'textbook' buy at 90 was downgraded to a looser 80-90 accumulation, price slid below 90, the stop was swept, and a deeper ~77 zone is now being monitored — so the entry has drifted lower and conviction has fallen, even though the direction stayed bullish.
plain_english: Both reads from the same source point the same way — buy Litecoin cheap and hold for a move up toward 115, then 124, with a longer-term stretch to around 150. But the picture got weaker over time: by mid-June price had dropped under the planned 90 buy area, the safety stop had already been triggered, and the trader started watching a lower ~77 zone for a possible deeper dip first. So don't chase it here — wait for price to come down into the 77-80 accumulation band, and only stay in while it holds above about 75 on a daily closing basis.
